commit.message:
feat: Teilentnahme per Swipe-Right auf Artikeln

- Neue Komponente RemoveQuantitySheet mit Stepper (+/- Buttons)
- Swipe nach rechts auf ItemRow zeigt grünes 'Entnehmen'-Feld
- Sheet zeigt aktuelle Menge, Entnahme-Stepper, verbleibende Menge
- Bei Restmenge 0: Bestätigungsdialog zum Löschen des Artikels
- Nur bei Artikeln mit Menge > 1 verfügbar

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: skip E2B on FLAGSHIP, disable broken SM8550 embedding variant (#205)

* fix: skip E2B on FLAGSHIP, disable broken SM8550 embedding variant

- ModelDownloadManager.init now skips GEMMA_4_E2B on FLAGSHIP tier — E4B
  is auto-queued via the tier-optional loop instead, preventing both from
  downloading simultaneously with E2B finishing first
- areRequiredModelsDownloaded() accepts E4B OR E2B on FLAGSHIP so the chat
  screen doesn't show ModelsNotReady after E4B downloads
- EMBEDDING_GEMMA_300M_SM8550.preferredForTier = null (was FLAGSHIP) — the
  SM8550 .tflite variant fails with "Unsupported file format" on device
- OnboardingViewModel.preferredEmbeddingModel always uses the generic
  EMBEDDING_GEMMA_300M until the SM8550 variant is fixed upstream

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: correct FLAGSHIP download progress tracking, remove broken SM8550 embedding candidate

- ModelDownloadManager exposes deviceTier property
- ChatViewModel builds displayModels list from FLAGSHIP-aware logic (E4B
  included, E2B excluded) so anyDownloading and progress are correct during
  E4B download on S23 Ultra
- LiteRtEmbeddingEngine.modelFileCandidates() no longer tries SM8550 file
- One-shot cleanup deletes SM8550 file on cold start if present on disk

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: move SM8550 cleanup to Dispatchers.IO — no disk I/O on main thread

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: NickMonrad <nick@lab3.com.au>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
CRPS: Contrastive Reasoning Path Synthesis

Complete implementation of the CRPS framework:
- MCTS exploration with batch search (Qwen2.5-Math-7B-Instruct explorer)
- Distribution-aware contrastive pair sampling (hard/soft negatives)
- Dual-granularity contrastive analysis (gpt-5-mini analyst)
- Pattern-informed path synthesis with verification
- SFT training pipeline (DeepSpeed ZeRO-3)
- vLLM-based evaluation with SymPy answer verification

commit.message:
feat: reconnect to stored outbound peers on startup (#313)

* Initial plan

* feat: connect to stored outbound peers on startup

- Add get_outbound_peers(limit) to storage/core.rs to query peers with
  non-null multiaddr ordered by last_seen DESC
- Only persist multiaddr for outbound (Dialer) connections in
  event_processor.rs; inbound Listener connections store None
- After swarm.listen_on in main.rs, dial up to 10 stored outbound peers
  at startup so previously-connected peers reconnect automatically
- Add three unit tests for get_outbound_peers
- Update CHANGELOG.md under Unreleased

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/bhagdave/p2p-play/sessions/704a0da3-7b8b-4f4e-9e7a-948707ec26b1

Co-authored-by: bhagdave <3230037+bhagdave@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: correct spelling of multiaddrs in test comments

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/bhagdave/p2p-play/sessions/704a0da3-7b8b-4f4e-9e7a-948707ec26b1

Co-authored-by: bhagdave <3230037+bhagdave@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: address reviewer feedback on get_outbound_peers and startup logging

- Use i64::try_from(limit) instead of limit as i64 in get_outbound_peers
- Propagate row-mapping errors via collect::<Result<Vec<_>,_>>() instead
  of silently dropping them with filter_map
- Add test_get_outbound_peers_orders_by_last_seen_desc to verify ORDER BY
  last_seen DESC behavior with deterministic timestamps
- Surface multiaddr parse errors and DB load failures in the TUI via
  app.add_to_log in addition to the error! log call

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/bhagdave/p2p-play/sessions/fedd686c-c765-477d-afdf-2260a603eed7

repository.description: :blue_book: Home Assistant User documentation

commit.message:
fix: spawn orientation from SpawnPoint rotation + RL reward/epsilon overhaul

Spawn orientation:
- getDefaultSpawnPos now returns rotX/Y/Z/W from the SpawnPoint object
- spawnCurrentVehicleType uses spawn point rotation (not current vehicle rotation)
- Extracted spawnObjToResult() helper to avoid duplicate pos+rot extraction

RL learning fixes:
- CoverageMap now tracks ep_visited (per-episode) separately from total (global)
- Reward structure:
    +10  brand-new cell (global first visit)
    + 1  first visit this episode on known terrain -> follow learned routes
    -0.5 loop within same episode
  This ensures the route to the wall and beyond stays positively rewarded
- EPSILON_DECAY 0.993 -> 0.95: at ep50 epsilon is now ~0.07 not ~0.70
- STUCK_PATIENCE 25 -> 50: less false stuck detections
- Map display: shows ep_visited (solid) vs global-only (░) vs unknown (.)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(nav): make Progress and Analytics screens accessible from Profile (#520)

Progress and Analytics screens existed but had no navigation entry point.
Users couldn't access these key features from anywhere in the app UI.

Changes:
- Add "Progress & Stats" section to ProfileScreen with two items:
  - "Progress" -> navigates to progress route (charts, PRs, plateau alerts)
  - "Analytics" -> navigates to analytics route (volume, consistency, insights)
- Wire navigation callbacks in ProfileRoute and GymBroNavGraph
- Add bilingual strings (EN + ES) for labels and subtitles
- Use ShowChart and BarChart icons with AccentAmberStart color
- Position prominently after AI Coach button (key feature visibility)

Progress screen links to Analytics, which now has two entry points:
- From Profile -> Progress -> Analytics (existing)
- From Profile -> Analytics (new direct access)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <copilot@github.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
refactor: modernize test assertions and infrastructure (#12982)

Migrate all legacy NUnit assertions to AwesomeAssertions .Should() style,
add NUnit.Analyzers for static analysis, and clean up test infrastructure.

Assertion migration:
- Convert ~1,900 ClassicAssert/Assert.That/CollectionAssert/StringAssert
  calls across 101 files to AwesomeAssertions .Should() fluent style
- Delete unused AssertEx helper (CommonTestUtils/AssertEx.cs)
- Add AwesomeAssertions dependency to CommonTestUtils.csproj

Infrastructure:
- Add NUnit.Analyzers 4.7.0 to Directory.Packages.props and Tests.targets
- Ban ClassicAssert, CollectionAssert, StringAssert via BannedSymbols.txt
- Remove global 'using NUnit.Framework.Legacy' from tests/Directory.Build.props
- Fix all NUnit analyzer warnings (NUnit1001/1002/1031/1032):
  - Make test parameters nullable where null TestCase values are used
  - Use nameof() in TestCaseSource attributes
  - Add TearDown disposal for IDisposable fields

Cleanup:
- Remove redundant [TestFixture] attributes from all 214 test classes
  (NUnit 4 auto-discovers test classes without this attribute)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Pre-build libbearssl.a instead of compiling per release

Added 'Build BearSSL' workflow (manual dispatch) that:
- Fetches the musl GCC toolchain (same one shipped in releases)
- Compiles all 293 BearSSL sources into libbearssl.a
- Commits the result to packaging/prebuilt/linux-x86_64/

Release workflow now just copies the pre-built library into the
bundle instead of compiling it. Saves several minutes per release.

Run the Build BearSSL workflow once to populate the library,
then again whenever the BearSSL submodule is updated.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Close GC gate and add dual-mode WASI runner (#7)

* Close Phase 11 GC gate

Sync the Phase 11 runtime-stability documents with the collector-backed gc-metrics artifact so Gate 2 is marked done and the remaining selfhosting mainline status matches the current CI evidence.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add dual-mode WASI runner

Introduce a WasiMode-based runner dispatcher for preview1 core Wasm and preview2 components, and route LSHARP_PATH wasm/component delegation through the shared entrypoint.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add bike helmet analysis site and README

Mobile-friendly shopping guide for children's bike helmets in Norway.
Based on scientific tests from Trygg Trafikk/RISE/KTH (2025),
Folksam (2025), Virginia Tech, and ADAC/Stiftung Warentest.

Features:
- Responsive HTML page optimized for mobile in-store use
- Collapsible sections for each child (boy 49cm, girl 52cm)
- Top 3 ranked helmets per child with colour swatches
- Interactive fit checklist with checkboxes
- Budget overview and Norwegian store links
- Full README with test data and source links

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(cli): replace hardcoded state lists with dynamic directory scan (#908)

- Dynamic listSync() scan replaces static STATE_DIRS/STATE_FILES arrays
- Eliminates silent orphaning when new state artifacts are added
- KEEP_LOCAL protects 5 files read from the working tree by runtime code
  that does not go through external-state resolution:
  config.json, manifest.json, workstreams.json, upstream.json,
  squad-registry.json
- Add 6 tests for runExternalize/runInternalize covering basic ops,
  dynamic scan of unknown entries, config preservation, round-trip,
  config cleanup, and empty .squad/ edge case

Closes #863

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(watch): detect rate-limit errors instead of reporting Board is clear (#893)

* fix(watch): detect rate-limit errors

* fix(watch): narrow rate-limit detection to 429 and explicit rate-limit strings

Remove broad 403 match that misclassified auth errors as rate limits.
A bare 403 Forbidden (e.g. insufficient token scopes) is a permanent auth
error, not a transient rate limit. Now only matches 429 status codes and
explicit rate-limit message strings.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Dina Berry <diberry@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
refactor: consolidate engine into codenook-init

Architecture change: single global skill instead of two.

- Move hitl-adapters/ from codenook-engine to codenook-init
- Convert engine SKILL.md to templates/codenook.instructions.md
  (deployed per-project, auto-loaded by platform)
- Remove codenook-engine directory entirely
- Update install.sh: installs only codenook-init, auto-cleans old engine
- Update codenook-init SKILL.md: Step 4 now copies hitl-adapters and
  writes instructions file from template

Before: 2 global skills (codenook-init + codenook-engine)
After:  1 global skill (codenook-init), engine lives in project instructions

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: use !errorlevel! and !MODE! for reliable delayed expansion in START.bat

- set MODE/MAPCHOICE with !errorlevel! (runtime expansion, not parse-time)
- All comparisons now use !var! instead of %var%
- Replaced if %MODE%==2 (...) block with if !MODE!==1 goto :install_mod
  (blocks freeze %var% at parse time, breaking set /p inside them)
- Added missing :install_mod label so goto doesn't skip to EOF
- All MAP references now use !MAP! consistently

commit.message:
Add BaseResearchAgent, DomainConfig, and engine framework (v0.2.0) (#5)

* Add BaseResearchAgent, DomainConfig, and engine framework (v0.2.0)

Evolves researcher-lib from a set of extracted primitives into the SDK
for building domain-scoped research agents on the khonliang platform.

New modules:

BaseResearchAgent (agent.py):
- Extends BaseAgent from bus-lib — bus connectivity inherited
- ~20 generic research skills with @handler implementations:
  search, distill, synthesize, explore, ideas, concept bundling
- Pipeline wiring from config (stores, models, embedder, synthesizer)
- DomainConfig integration (rules, engines, prompts loaded at startup)
- synergize_concepts returns concept bundles, NOT FRs (FR generation
  is developer's job, not the librarian's)
- No FR tools, no code-specific tools, no pipeline management tools
  (those belong in domain extensions, not the generic base)

DomainConfig (domain.py):
- Declares how a domain differs from generic: evaluation rules,
  search engines, prompt overrides, output types, knowledge sources
- from_yaml, from_dict, generic() constructors
- load_prompt resolves file paths or inline strings
- rules_prompt_fragment for LLM prompt injection

Engine framework (engines.py):
- BaseSearchEngine ABC + EngineRegistry with config-driven selection
- SearchResult dataclass for unified engine output
- WebSearchEngine (DuckDuckGo, no API key) and WebFetchEngine (URL fetch)
  ship as defaults — domain engines live in their own packages

Updated:
- pyproject.toml: version 0.2.0, added khonliang-bus-lib dependency
- __init__.py: exports all new types, updated module docstring

Tests: 34 new tests (168 total, all pass)
- test_domain: config loading, YAML parsing, prompt resolution, edge cases
- test_engines: registry, search routing, error handling, source tagging
- test_agent: skill registration, FR/code/pipeline skill exclusion, domain config

Spec: specs/researcher-lib-evolution.md — full plan for v0.2-v0.4

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Address Copilot review feedback on PR #5

15 inline comments addressed:

engines.py:
- BaseException not Exception for asyncio.gather return_exceptions (CancelledError is BaseException in 3.11+)
- WebFetchEngine now returns full extracted text in snippet, not truncated 500 chars. Preview stored in metadata.

domain.py:
- from_dict accepts Optional[dict] with explicit None handling
- is_generic checks all specialization fields (prompts, output_type, knowledge_sources, relevance_keywords) not just name/rules/engines
- Engine name in docstring: "google" → "web_search" (matches actual engine name)

agent.py:
- Removed unused SynthesisResult import
- Skill schemas: removed detail params from handlers that don't implement them (find_relevant, paper_context, synthesize_topic/project, score_relevance)
- Skill schemas: added min_score and limit params to concepts_for_project (handler reads them)
- Removed batch_size from start_distillation schema (base impl is a no-op)
- fetch_paper stores full content not just snippet
- Engine name in docstrings: "google" → "web_search"

__init__.py:
- Engine name in example: "google" → "web_search"

tests:
- Removed unused imports (AsyncMock/MagicMock/patch from test_agent, Path from test_domain)

specs:
- Updated engine names throughout (GoogleEngine → WebSearchEngine, "google" → "web_search")

SSRF note (engines.py:249): valid concern for networked deployment, not blocking for local-only. Logged for future consideration when agents serve external users.

168 tests pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Remove unused pytest import from test_domain.py (complete review feedback #14)

commit.message:
Fix cathode Z sign and remove redundant void cast

- SteppingAction.cc: cathode face Z = -(halfTPCLen) + cathThick/2
  (was incorrectly subtracting instead of adding the half-thickness offset)
- RunAction.cc: remove unnecessary (void)nev; nev is used in the G4cout
  statement in the non-ROOT build path

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: WenjieWu-Sci <17972563+WenjieWu-Sci@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix BearSSL: compile with musl GCC and fix link order

Two fixes for TLS compilation on Linux:

1. Release CI: compile libbearssl.a with the bundled musl GCC
   (not system glibc GCC) so the library matches the shipped compiler.
   Merged the separate Build/Inject steps into one step that runs
   after toolchain assembly.

2. Link order: move libbearssl.a after the source file on the
   command line. GCC's linker processes left-to-right — archives
   before the objects that reference them get their symbols discarded.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add complete Geant4 gas TPC simulation project

- CMakeLists.txt: cmake 3.16+, Geant4 (required) + ROOT (optional),
  file-glob sources, copies macros/config/gdml to build dir
- include/ & src/: 11 classes covering the full simulation chain:
    ConfigManager   – flat-JSON config parser (no external deps)
    GasProperties   – Xe / SF6 / SeF6 parameters (W, Fano, attachment)
    IonizationModel – Fano fluctuations + exponential e⁻ attachment
    ElectricField   – G4ElectroMagneticField; uniform field along Z
    DetectorConstruction – cylindrical TPC (builtin C++) or GDML mode;
                           applies E-field to 'DriftRegion' logical volume
    PhysicsList     – G4EmStandardPhysics_option4 + G4DecayPhysics
    PrimaryGeneratorAction – G4ParticleGun configured from JSON
    TPCHit          – G4VHit with G4Allocator
    EventAction     – collects TPCHits per event
    RunAction       – ROOT TTree output (HAS_ROOT) or CSV fallback
    SteppingAction  – records deposits, calls IonizationModel
- macros/run_builtin.mac, run_gdml.mac
- config/detector.json, gas.json, field.json
- gdml/simple_tpc.gdml – valid GDML cylindrical TPC (Xe, Cu electrodes)
- README.md updated with build/run instructions and parameter tables

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: WenjieWu-Sci <17972563+WenjieWu-Sci@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: complete services and controllers overhaul

- emailService: full rewrite with sendOtpEmail, sendPasswordResetEmail,
  sendWelcomeEmail, sendBookingConfirmation, sendDataBackupEmail,
  sendAlertEmail; graceful SMTP fallback logs with 📧 EMAIL prefix

- pdfService: professional PDFKit-based invoice + report generation
  with header banners, tables, totals, footers, page breaks

- excelService: ExcelJS rewrite with toBuffer, exportBookings,
  exportGuests, exportReport; styled headers, alternating row shading

- aiService (new): in-process AI engine with predictRevenue (linear
  regression + day-of-week seasonal adjustment), predictOccupancy
  (7-day moving average), getGuestRecommendations (collaborative
  filtering), detectAnomalies (z-score), optimizePricing (rule-based
  multipliers), analyzeSentiment (lexicon-based), clusterGuests
  (k-means++, k=3)

- automationService (new): scheduled workflows with scheduleAll/stopAll/
  getStatus; runDailyReport, runMonthlyInvoiceReminder, runRoomStatusSync,
  runDatabaseBackup, runAnomalyCheck; NOT auto-started on require

- jarvisController: rewritten to use aiService; exports forecast,
  detectAnomaly, getInsights, optimizePricing, getRecommendations,
  analyzeSentiment

- analyticsController: added getGuestAnalytics, getBookingSourceAnalytics,
  getRoomPerformance, getForecast; dual MongoDB/JSON-DB support

- reportsController: added getDailyReport, getMonthlyReport,
  getAuditReport, exportReport; dual MongoDB/JSON-DB support

- pdfController: added generateReport endpoint

- invoiceController: added markAsPaid, markAsCancelled

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: use choice command for reliable mode/map selection in START.bat

Replaced unreliable 'set /p' string comparisons with 'choice' command
which sets ERRORLEVEL directly - no string parsing issues.
- MODE 1 = WASD, MODE 2 = RL
- if/goto routing now uses numeric ERRORLEVEL values
- Fixed if-block parentheses issue in :run_rl

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: update HITL adapter paths from .agents/ to codenook/

All 4 adapters (terminal, local-html, github-issue, confluence) now
auto-detect platform root (.github/codenook/ or .claude/codenook/)
instead of using hardcoded .agents/ paths.
